papers.title_en: PatternGSL: A Structured Specification Language for Template-Free and Simulation-Ready 3D Garments
papers.authors: Zhenyang Li, Lutao Jiang, Yizhou Zhao, Ying-Cong Chen, Xin Wang, Weikai Chen, Yifan Peng
papers.evidence: 从单张图像重建3D服装的可学习规格语言
papers.tldr: 单图像3D服装重建面临模板无关方法缺乏缝制结构、模板方法受限于预定义模板的困境。为此提出PatternGSL，一种可学习的规范语言，以紧凑标准化形式编码完整缝制图案包括面板边界、参数化接缝和缝合拓扑，实现无模板且模拟就绪。同时构建视觉语言框架直接预测PatternGSL并解码，无需优化。大规模数据集PatternGSLData支持训练，实验证明精度提升、缝制结构恢复和可靠仿真。
papers.abstract_en: Reconstructing realistic, physically plausible garments from a single image remains a fundamental challenge. Template-free methods capture surface geometry but lack explicit sewing structure for simulation; while programmatic systems are simulation-ready but constrained by predefined templates. This reveals a fundamental representation gap between geometric reconstruction and structured garment construction. We present PatternGSL, a structured garment representation in the form of a template-free and learnable specification language that encodes complete sewing patterns, including panel boundaries, parameterized seams, and explicit stitch topology, in a compact and standardized form. PatternGSL preserves the physical rigor of pattern-based models while removing template dependence, elevating sewing structure as a first-class target for generative modeling. We further propose a vision-language framework that predicts PatternGSL specifications directly from a single image and decodes them into garments using lightweight deterministic validity handling, without optimization-based refinement or manual cleanup. In addition, we introduce PatternGSLData, the first large-scale image-to-GSL paired dataset comprising 300K samples with complete sewing pattern annotations, enabling supervised VLM training for structured garment reconstruction. Experiments demonstrate improved pattern accuracy over prior baselines, explicit sewing-structure recovery, reliable cloth simulation, and pattern-level editing through the same deterministic decoding pipeline. Code and data-processing scripts will be released at https://github.com/PatternGSL/PatternGSL.

papers.title_en: PatternGSL: A Structured Specification Language for Template-Free and Simulation-Ready 3D Garments
papers.authors: Zhenyang Li, Lutao Jiang, Yizhou Zhao, Ying-Cong Chen, Xin Wang, Weikai Chen, Yifan Peng
papers.evidence: 单图3D服装重建，使用结构化版型语言
papers.tldr: 针对单图服装重建中几何与缝纫结构间的表示鸿沟，提出PatternGSL——一种无模板、可学习的结构化规范语言，能编码完整缝纫图案与缝合拓扑。结合视觉语言框架，直接从单图预测PatternGSL并解码为仿真服装。构建30万样本数据集PatternGSLData。实验表明，该方法在图案准确性、缝纫结构恢复、布料模拟及图案级编辑上显著优于现有基线。
papers.abstract_en: Reconstructing realistic, physically plausible garments from a single image remains a fundamental challenge. Template-free methods capture surface geometry but lack explicit sewing structure for simulation; while programmatic systems are simulation-ready but constrained by predefined templates. This reveals a fundamental representation gap between geometric reconstruction and structured garment construction. We present PatternGSL, a structured garment representation in the form of a template-free and learnable specification language that encodes complete sewing patterns, including panel boundaries, parameterized seams, and explicit stitch topology, in a compact and standardized form. PatternGSL preserves the physical rigor of pattern-based models while removing template dependence, elevating sewing structure as a first-class target for generative modeling. We further propose a vision-language framework that predicts PatternGSL specifications directly from a single image and decodes them into garments using lightweight deterministic validity handling, without optimization-based refinement or manual cleanup. In addition, we introduce PatternGSLData, the first large-scale image-to-GSL paired dataset comprising 300K samples with complete sewing pattern annotations, enabling supervised VLM training for structured garment reconstruction. Experiments demonstrate improved pattern accuracy over prior baselines, explicit sewing-structure recovery, reliable cloth simulation, and pattern-level editing through the same deterministic decoding pipeline. Code and data-processing scripts will be released at https://github.com/PatternGSL/PatternGSL.

papers.title_en: Extracting Neural Materials from Multi-view Images
papers.authors: Kim Youwang, Jon Hasselgren, Peter Kocsis, Andrea Weidlich, Tae-Hyun Oh, Jacob Munkberg
papers.evidence: 可微逆渲染提取神经材质，结合大材质重建模型
papers.tldr: 神经材质可紧凑表达复杂反射，但获取困难。本文提出NeuMatEx，训练大型材质重建模型LMRM预测初始材质和不确定性，再用可微逆路径追踪优化。高置信区域锚定预测防止光照烘焙。实验表明比PBR方法更好质量和分解。贡献在于解决神经材质优化局部最优问题。
papers.abstract_en: Neural materials can represent complex specular reflections and scattering effects in a compact, universal basis. However, acquiring and authoring such materials remains challenging. We present NeuMatEx, a differentiable inverse rendering method for extracting spatially varying neural materials from images. The nonlinear structure of neural material latent spaces makes optimization with naive inverse rendering infeasible. To address this, we train a Large Material Reconstruction Model (LMRM) that directly predicts initialbase color, neural material latents, and aleatoric uncertainty guides from images. This material prior provides a good initialization and better constrains our subsequent optimization using inverse path tracing. The predicted uncertainty further helps by anchoring high-confidence regions more tightly to the LMRM prediction, preventing lighting and complex specular effects from being baked into materials. Experiments on synthetic and real assets show that NeuMatEx extracts complex materials with better visual quality and material decomposition than PBR-based methods.
